title: FailureRecoveryFrame (v2 stdlib derivation)
description: A stereotyped failure-recovery template per Fillmore's Frame Semantics. Each manifest binds the four canonical frame elements (Action_Attempted / Error_Type / Corrective_Action / Final_Outcome) to a specific recovery pattern an agent should follow when matching failures occur. Derivation: compose(Process[recovery_procedure], Policy[trigger_conditions], Scope[applicable_scope]). Frames are the procedural-memory unit Powerloom agents reach for when a tool call, validation, or external request fails — authored by operators at v057, distilled from episodic runs at v058+ via consolidation.

error_category.description: Coarse categorical label for the failure. Frames matching 'other' must populate error_type.signature for disambiguation.

expected_outcome.description: The Final_Outcome slot of the frame: what terminal state this template aims to reach when its corrective action runs successfully. Instances (Events) at v058+ report the *actual* outcome separately.

applicable_scope_ref.description: Scope inside which this frame can be invoked. Frames are scope-attached so the same error category can have different recovery patterns at org root vs. a specific OU.

applies_to_processes.description: Optional process refs (Agent / Skill / Workflow / MCPDeployment names) this frame is registered on. Empty = applies to any process within applicable_scope_ref.

action_attempted.description: Frame element 1: the kind of action this frame matches. summary is required free text; process_kind narrows by Process primitive's process_kind field if set; matcher_pattern is an optional regex applied to the action's structured payload.

process_kind.description: Optional narrowing: only match actions whose enclosing Process has this process_kind (e.g., 'agent_runtime', 'skill_invocation', 'mcp_server').

matcher_pattern.description: Optional regex applied to the action's serialized payload at match time. Engine compiles + caches.

error_type.description: Frame element 2: the failure category this frame matches. category is required; code and signature narrow further.

signature.description: Optional regex matched against the error message. Required when category='other'.

corrective_action.description: Frame element 3: the procedure to run when a matching failure occurs. summary is required free text; steps is an optional ordered list; policy_ref optionally points to a Policy that constrains execution (e.g., max_retries, backoff schedule).

policy_ref.description: Optional Policy resource constraining the corrective action (e.g., max_retries, backoff_seconds, circuit_breaker_after).

max_attempts.description: Hard cap on how many times this frame's corrective action runs for a single triggering failure before escalation.

escalation_ref.description: Optional FailureRecoveryFrame name to invoke when this frame's max_attempts is exhausted without reaching final_outcome. Cycles are detected at apply time.

provenance.description: Where this frame came from. v057 ships only operator_authored; distilled_from_episodic lights up at v058+ when consolidation can promote observation conventions into frames.

evidence_run_ids.description: Optional list of session/workflow_run UUIDs the frame was distilled from. Empty for operator_authored frames at v057.
